_source: Dong et al. (2019) J Sustainable Water in the Built Environment 5(4):04019005 — HYDRUS CW2D constructed wetland modeling
_note: Validates Richards equation solver with constructed wetland media parameters from HYDRUS CW2D

_provenance.method: van Genuchten-Mualem parameters from HYDRUS CW2D standard databases + Šimůnek et al. (2012)
_provenance.reproduction_note: Re-run baseline_command at baseline_commit to regenerate expected values
references: Dong et al. (2019) J Sustainable Water in the Built Environment 5(4):04019005
Šimůnek et al. (2012) HYDRUS software for simulating the movement of water, heat, and multiple solutes in variably saturated media
_provenance._tolerance_justification: VG retention tolerance 0.005 covers f64 rounding. K ratio ranges span one order of magnitude around computed value to accommodate Mualem model sensitivity. Solver checks are boolean pass/fail. Mass balance at 50% accommodates coarse time stepping on fast-draining gravel.

gravel.source: HYDRUS CW2D standard gravel media (Šimůnek et al. 2012)

organic_substrate.source: HYDRUS CW2D organic layer / compost mix

fine_gravel.source: HYDRUS CW2D fine gravel / pea gravel

cw2d_retention_curves.description: VG water retention for CW2D media at key pressure heads

cw2d_conductivity.description: Mualem-VG hydraulic conductivity for CW2D media

cw2d_gravel_infiltration.description: 1D infiltration into initially dry gravel — extremely fast drainage

cw2d_organic_drainage.description: Free drainage from initially wet organic substrate — high retention slows drainage

checks.description: Average θ remains above θr + 0.1 due to organic retention

cw2d_mass_balance.description: Mass balance closure for CW2D media simulations

test_cases._note: Gravel Ks=5000 cm/d drains nearly instantly; simplified mass balance check approaches 100% when initial and final states are both near residual θ
